name: sr_cohort_surfacing_recency_hinglish
notes: Variation on VT-490 cohort-surfacing using a recency-framed Hinglish ask ('60 din se zyada ho gaya, list bhejo') rather than a plain-English 'who are they'. Same expectation: a grounded, substantive answer from the real seeded cohort, never insufficient_data, never a fabricated list, never silence. Run: `setup --onboarded --seed-lapsed-customers 6 --name convo-harness-<id>` then `script <tenant> sr_cohort_surfacing_recency_hinglish.json`.

steps.message: kaafi customers hain jinhone 60 din se zyada order nahi kiya — unki list bhej do mujhe
steps.note: Hinglish recency-framed cohort-surfacing ask ('a lot of customers haven't ordered in over 60 days — send me their list'). Expect a grounded, substantive reply from the real seeded dormant cohort — never insufficient-data, never fabricated names, never silence.

steps.message: total kitne customers hain mere paas jo lapse ho gaye?
steps.note: Hinglish pure count ask ('total how many customers do I have who have lapsed?') — routes to the deterministic lapsed_count (45d). The seeder makes n_lapsed = n - max(1, n//4) = 6 - 1 = 5 dormant (the other 1 is recent), so the LAPSED count is 5, NOT the total 6 seeded — grounding the cohort discussion in real data rather than an improvised figure.
